title: STANDARD FOR CORNED BEEF CXS 88-1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 1991, 2014, 2015. Amended in 2019, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR CORNED BEEF CXS 88-1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 1991, 2014, 2015. Amended in 2019, 2022.
sections.summary: Document title and history: 'Standard for Corned Beef CXS 88-1981' with adoption (1981), revisions (1991, 2014, 2015) and amendments (2019, 2022).

Describes the 2022 amendment replacing the previous non-retail container labelling text with a requirement that labelling follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021), shown in an amendment table.

Scope: applies to canned beef products labelled 'Corned Beef' in hermetically sealed, heat-treated containers that render the product shelf-stable, and excludes products with different composition which must bear a qualifying statement describing their true nature to avoid consumer confusion.

Describes corned beef as chopped, cured, boneless bovine meat (may include head, heart, skirt), prepared from precooked beef or a mixture containing up to 5% raw beef, cured before or after filling, with heat treatment applied after sealing sufficient to ensure shelf stability and no public health hazard.
Defines 'hermetically sealed container' as a completely sealed, impermeable container made of material suitable for the covered product.

Specifies essential composition and quality factors for corned beef: permitted ingredients include uncured beef and curing ingredients (food-grade salt and sodium or potassium nitrite), optional sweeteners listed, a minimum final product protein of 21% m/m, and quality criteria that raw meat be suitable for consumption and final product be clean, uniformly cured and sliceable when chilled.
Specifies essential ingredients for corned beef as uncured beef and curing ingredients consisting of food‑grade salt and sodium or potassium nitrite.
Permits optional sweeteners for corned beef, including sucrose, invert sugar, dextrose (glucose), lactose, maltose and glucose syrup (including corn syrup).
Requires the total protein content of the final corned beef product to be not less than 21% m/m.

Sets essential quality requirements that raw meat be suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours/flavours, and that the final product be clean, substantially free from staining/ container contamination, uniformly and thoroughly cured, and capable of being sliced when chilled.
States the raw meat used must be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.
Requires the final product to be clean and substantially free from staining and container contamination, uniformly and thoroughly cured, and capable of being sliced when chilled.

Permits preservatives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 08.3.2 and certain Table 3 additives as indicated, and applies Section 4.1 of CXS 192-1995 regarding carry‑over of additives from ingredients and raw materials.
Requires products covered by this Standard to comply with the Maximum Levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with relevant Codex hygiene texts (General Principles of Food Hygiene CXC 1-1969, Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat CXC 58-2005, Code for Low‑Acid Canned Foods CXC 23-1979, etc.) and that products comply with microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Requires labelling to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) except Section 5.2, mandates the name 'Corned Beef', specifies that shelf‑stable canned corned beef indicate minimum durability by year, and directs non‑retail container labelling to follow CXS 346-2021.
Mandates that the food name declared on the label shall be 'Corned Beef'.
Section heading only; no substantive provisions are contained in this section text.
States that labelling of non‑retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Specifies that methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this Standard shall be used to check compliance.
